quotes.why_load_bearing: This is the method's core generative instruction for this section: it tells the student exactly how to extend all preceding fourth-interval patterns into triplet groupings, a technique that compounds the preceding exercises into a new layer of practice.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Practice the same pattern except start on B-natural and follow the whole-tone scale of B (instead
of B-flat).
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is a rule specifying how to transpose the whole-tone pattern to the B-natural scale, which is a distinct whole-tone set from B-flat and requires the student to apply the pattern to a different tonal universe.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Also start on B-natural and C and practice all of the pattern.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is a recurring instructional directive that extends each exercise across multiple starting pitches, embedding transposition practice as a required component of the method.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Also practice this pattern starting on B-natural, C, and D-flat (B augmented, C augmented, and D-flat augmented).
quotes.why_load_bearing: This identifies the three transposition roots for the augmented-chord pattern, making explicit that only three starting points are needed to cover all augmented triads due to their symmetrical interval structure.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Also practice pattern starting on E-natural (B-natural to E-natural) and F (C to F).
quotes.why_load_bearing: This rule specifies the additional roots needed to complete coverage of the diminished seventh pattern across the full symmetrical cycle.
